mission.title: minio: server: [gateway] Support for Backblaze B2
mission.description: server: [gateway] Support for Backblaze B2. This issue affects 9+ users.

steps.title: Identify minio troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your minio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n minio-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio-operator --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review minio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant minio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n minio-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl get configmap -n minio-operator -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=minio
```
Hello

I think a lot of user could find a S3 gateway for B2 very useful, as there is a lot of backups tools for S3 and B2 is very cheap (0,005$/GB).

steps.title: Apply the fix for server: [gateway] Support for Backblaze B2
steps.description:
Implement backbla
```yaml
msg="Unable to list buckets." cause="b2_list_buckets: 401: expired_auth_token: Authorization token has expired"
```

steps.title: Confirm server: [gateway] Support for Backblaze B2 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio-operator --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n minio-operator --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/minio/minio/pull/5002
codeSnippets: msg="Unable to list buckets." cause="b2_list_buckets: 401: expired_auth_token: Authorization token has expired"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with minio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
